annotation_reason: Qwen returned an approximate 3x3 product region; this is not a true bounding box.
report:
VisionGuard inspected 188 product images this shift and detected 79 potential defects, giving a defect rate of 42.0%.

The most common defect type was "crack" and the most common defect category was "surface crack". The highest severity observed was "critical". Average Qwen-VL inference latency was 1480ms per image on AMD MI300X.

The likely recurring causes include model uncertainty on a high-sensitivity inspection frame, visible anomaly may require human confirmation, quality-control policy prevents silent release of uncertain items. Recommended action: pause the affected line, inspect the nearby batch, and review process settings before continuing full-speed production. Prevention summary: collect confirmed defect examples for factory-specific adaptation, improve camera lighting and product positioning, use operator feedback to tune inspection thresholds.
operations_alert:
Operations Alert:
Crack-related defects increased in the latest inspection batch.

Risk:
Critical or warning-level defects are concentrated on Line LINE-A1.

Most common likely cause:
model uncertainty on a high-sensitivity inspection frame, visible anomaly may require human confirmation, quality-control policy prevents silent release of uncertain items.

Recommended immediate action:
inspect nearby batch items, route item to operator review, inspect nearby batch items if the anomaly is confirmed.

Prevention:
collect confirmed defect examples for factory-specific adaptation, improve camera lighting and product positioning, use operator feedback to tune inspection thresholds.
